sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The initial frame is from level 1, not level 2. I need to find the first frame of level 2 in the history. Let me look through the history to find when the level changed.
Also, I notice the initial level 1 frame only has 6 blocks: 4 yellow blocks and 2 charcoal blocks. This is very different from level 2.
Let me find the transition from level 1 to level 2 in the history.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Find the first frame of level 2 in history
for i, entry in enumerate(history):
    if entry.frame.level == 2:
        print(f"First level 2 frame at history index {i}, step {entry.frame.step}")
        level2_frame = entry.frame
        break

# Get all blocks in first level 2 frame
blocks = []
for n in level2_frame.segmentation['nodes']:
    if n['pixels'] <= 10 and n['color'] != 'B':  # exclude background
        boundary = n['boundary']
        min_row = min(p[0] for p in boundary)
        min_col = min(p[1] for p in boundary)
        blocks.append((min_row, min_col, n['color'], n['pixels']))
blocks.sort()

print(f"\nLevel 2 initial blocks ({len(blocks)}):")
for b in blocks:
    print(f"  {b}")

# Separate by color
from collections import defaultdict
by_color = defaultdict(list)
for b in blocks:
    by_color[b[2]].append(b)

print(f"\nBy color:")
for color, blist in sorted(by_color.items()):
    print(f"  {color}: {len(blist)} blocks")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
